title: Kelsey Fitzsimmons was found not guilty. Now she’s suing.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover officer, was found not guilty of assault after being shot by a colleague.
Fitzsimmons plans to sue North Andover, alleging negligence and civil rights violations in handling the restraining order.
The incident involved her former fiancé, Justin Aylaian, and questions about police conduct during the restraining order process.

title: A church turned 6-BR home in Nahant — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A converted church in Nahant, originally built in 1851, now serves as a 6-bedroom home.
The property includes ocean views, an in-law apartment, and spans 5,969 square feet.
Potential listing prices for the home range from $1.6 million to $2 million.

title: Should I DIY repair damage to a brand-new vehicle?
author: John Paul
bullets: DIY repairs on a damaged side mirror of a leased 2026 Honda CR-V are possible but may affect lease obligations.
Robust electronics in side mirrors are typically not affected by weather damage, but color matching paint is available for DIY repairs.
Car Doctor John Paul suggests that significant new car damage may warrant professional repairs rather than DIY efforts.

title: A nude man was seen walking around a N.H. town overnight. Police would like to know who he is.
author: John Waller
bullets: Police in Bedford, N.H., are seeking a man seen walking nude in neighborhoods early Thursday morning.
The man reportedly walked up driveways between 2 a.m. and 3 a.m.; police found an open door but no occupants.
Similar sightings of a nude man were reported twice in the past two weeks on Campbell Road.

title: This nurse is running Boston for MGH — and to honor her grandfather
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Zerbey, a nurse from North Andover, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her grandfather.
Zerbey works at Massachusetts General Hospital, where her grandfather received treatment for pancreatic cancer.
She aims to raise funds for MGH's patient care, pediatric cancer research, and home base program through her marathon run.

title: Heat coach Erik Spoelstra credited Celtics’ ‘exceptional level of shot-making’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Erik Spoelstra credited the Celtics' exceptional shooting in their 147-129 victory over the Heat on Wednesday.
The Celtics set a team record with 53 first-quarter points, heavily featuring Jaylen Brown and Sam Hauser's performances.
Despite a strong defensive effort, Spoelstra noted Boston's high shooting efficiency, particularly inside the 3-point line.

title: 9 of the best baseball movies streaming now
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: 'Field of Dreams' and 'Eephus' are among nine top baseball movies currently streaming.
Massachusetts-filmed 'Eephus' received a 100% Rotten Tomatoes rating and features Bill Lee.
Notable films missing from the streaming list include 'Moneyball' and 'Bang the Drum Slowly'.

title: Mass. man charged with threatening to kill Trump, ‘hang him from the Statue of Liberty’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Andrew Emerald, 45, arrested for threatening President Trump in Facebook posts, also drew a sword on FBI agents.
Emerald was indicted on eight counts for interstate transmission of threatening communications, according to federal prosecutors.
If convicted, he faces up to five years imprisonment per count, plus fines and supervised release, according to the U.S. attorney's office.

title: 15-year-old Charlestown girl reported missing
author: John Waller
bullets: Hayden Fletcher, 15, was last seen on Monday at 5:30 a.m. near 13th Street in Charlestown.
Fletcher is described as a 5-foot-4 white female, 128 pounds, with blue eyes and dark blond hair.
Boston police urge anyone with information to call 911 or Downtown Detectives at 617-343-4571.

title: Stoneham police arrest Watertown man for alleged indecent exposure at Michaels craft store
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Watertown resident Andrey Pavlov, 44, faced charges for alleged indecent exposure at two Stoneham businesses on Main Street.
Pavlov was arrested by Stoneham police and charged with open and gross lewdness, pleading not guilty in Woburn District Court.
One incident reportedly occurred at a Michaels craft store; the Stoneham Police acknowledged assistance from Watertown authorities.
